crime.value: Murder by poisoning - Derues killed victims and disposed of their bodies, with the central question being how he concealed the murders through elaborate deception
crime.quote: he was now compelled to follow that fatal law of crime which demands that blood must be effaced with blood

victim.value: Madame de Lamotte and her son Edouard - they were killable because they possessed property/wealth that Derues coveted and could access through his business relationship with them
victim.quote: Edouard de Lamotte, loving his mother as much as she loved him, became uneasy at receiving no visits

closed_world.value: The social and business circles of 18th century France, bounded by Derues' network of acquaintances, properties, and the limited communication methods of the era
closed_world.quote: she has been at Versailles, endeavouring to obtain your admission as a royal page

culprit_and_motive.value: Derues committed the murders for financial gain - to steal property and wealth from the Lamotte family through fraudulent business dealings
culprit_and_motive.quote: that fatal law of crime which demands that blood must be effaced with blood

resolution.value: Derues was captured, tried, and executed by being broken on the wheel, then burned - justice restored through official legal proceedings
resolution.quote: the executioner gave the signal. One would have thought a very few blows would have finished so frail a being

suspect_architecture.value: Single primary perpetrator (Derues) with wife as accomplice - crimes span multiple victims (Madame de Lamotte, her son Edouard) with elaborate deception rather than multiple suspects with competing motives
suspect_architecture.quote: In 1777, Madame Derues was sentenced to perpetual imprisonment, and confined at the Salpetriere.

red_herring.value: Derues' elaborate deception about Madame de Lamotte arranging for her son to become a royal page at Versailles - a complete fabrication to explain her absence after murder
red_herring.quote: for the last ten days has been at Versailles, endeavouring to obtain your admission as a royal page

social_world.value: Religious educational hierarchy (reverend fathers, black-cassocked overseers) and aristocratic connections (royal pages, bishops, Hotel de Ville) - institutional power structures that Derues exploits for credibility
social_world.quote: you will there see the bishop, on whom I had the honour of waiting when I was there last

alibi.value: Derues constructs elaborate false narrative about victim's whereabouts - claiming she is at Versailles on official business when she is actually dead
alibi.quote: she wished to have the pleasure of telling you herself. Therefore, once again, when you see her

media_and_audience.value: Public spectacle of execution with crowd participation - people applauding during proceedings and buying bone fragments as lottery talismans afterward
media_and_audience.quote: While the execution was proceeding the people applauded. On the morrow they bought up the fragments
